contentBlocks.original: ॐ अस्य श्री आपदुद्धारक हनुमत् स्तोत्र महामन्त्र कवचस्य, विभीषण ऋषिः, हनुमान् देवता, सर्वापदुद्धारक श्रीहनुमत्प्रसादेन मम सर्वापन्निवृत्त्यर्थे, सर्वकार्यानुकूल्य सिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: ōṃ asya śrī āpaduddhāraka hanumat stōtra mahāmantra kavachasya, vibhīṣaṇa ṛṣiḥ, hanumān dēvatā, sarvāpaduddhāraka śrīhanumatprasādēna mama sarvāpannivṛttyarthē, sarvakāryānukūlya siddhyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Dedication (Viniyoga): Of this Śrī Āpaduddhāraka Hanumat Stōtra Mahāmantra Kavacham: the seer is Vibhīṣaṇa; the deity is Hanumān. Its application is declared for the removal of all calamities and the accomplishment of all purposes by the grace of Śrī Hanumān.

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यानम्
वामे करे वैरिभिदं वहन्तं
शैलं परे शृङ्खलहारिटङ्कम् ।
दधानमच्छच्छवियज्ञसूत्रं
भजे ज्वलत्कुण्डलमाञ्जनेयम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyānam
vāmē karē vairibhidaṃ vahantaṃ
śailaṃ parē śṛṅkhalahāriṭaṅkam ।
dadhānamachChachChaviyajñasūtraṃ
bhajē jvalatkuṇḍalamāñjanēyam ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Meditation (Dhyānam): I worship Āñjanēya — who holds in his left hand a mountain that crushes enemies, in his other hand the chisel that breaks chains, who wears the spotless pure sacred thread, and whose earrings blaze with radiance. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
संवीतकौपीन मुदञ्चिताङ्गुलिं
समुज्ज्वलन्मौञ्जिमथोपवीतिनम् ।
सकुण्डलं लम्बिशिखासमावृतं
तमाञ्जनेयं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃvītakaupīna mudañchitāṅguḻiṃ
samujjvalanmauñjimathōpavītinam ।
sakuṇḍalaṃ lambiśikhāsamāvṛtaṃ
tamāñjanēyaṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Wearing the loin-cloth, fingers upraised, shining with the muñja-grass cord and the upavīta (sacred thread), adorned with earrings, with flowing tuft of hair — to that Āñjanēya I surrender as my refuge. (2)

contentBlocks.original:
आपन्नाखिललोकार्तिहारिणे श्रीहनूमते ।
अकस्मादागतोत्पात नाशनाय नमो नमः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āpannākhilalōkārtihāriṇē śrīhanūmatē ।
akasmādāgatōtpāta nāśanāya namō namaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation upon salutation to Śrī Hanumān, the remover of the suffering of all the afflicted of the world — to the destroyer of sudden calamities and terrors. (3)
O remover of the sorrow, suffering, and fear of Śrī Rāma (caused by) his separation from Sītā — O destroyer of the triple affliction — O Āñjanēya, salutation to you. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
आधिव्याधि महामारी ग्रहपीडापहारिणे ।
प्राणापहर्त्रेदैत्यानां रामप्राणात्मने नमः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādhivyādhi mahāmārī grahapīḍāpahāriṇē ।
prāṇāpahartrēdaityānāṃ rāmaprāṇātmanē namaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the remover of mental distress, diseases, great epidemics and planetary afflictions — to the one who seizes the very life-breath of the demons — to the very soul of Rāma's life. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
संसारसागरावर्त कर्तव्यभ्रान्तचेतसाम् ।
शरणागतमर्त्यानां शरण्याय नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃsārasāgarāvarta kartavyabhrāntachētasām ।
śaraṇāgatamartyānāṃ śaraṇyāya namō'stu tē ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to you, O refuge of those mortals who have come seeking shelter — wandering in confusion about their duty in the whirlpool of worldly existence. (6)
Salutation to the one of diamond body, who is the fire of dissolution to the Rudra of all time, of immeasurable splendor — to this form of Rudra who paralyzed the Brahmāstra. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
रामेष्टं करुणापूर्णं हनूमन्तं भयापहम् ।
शत्रुनाशकरं भीमं सर्वाभीष्टप्रदायकम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmēṣṭaṃ karuṇāpūrṇaṃ hanūmantaṃ bhayāpaham ।
śatrunāśakaraṃ bhīmaṃ sarvābhīṣṭapradāyakam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The beloved of Rāma, full of compassion, Hanumān the remover of fear — the destroyer of enemies, the fierce one, the fulfiller of all desires — (8)

contentBlocks.original:
कारागृहे प्रयाणे वा सङ्ग्रामे शत्रुसङ्कटे ।
जले स्थले तथाऽऽकाशे वाहनेषु चतुष्पथे ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kārāgṛhē prayāṇē vā saṅgrāmē śatrusaṅkaṭē ।
jalē sthalē tathā''kāśē vāhanēṣu chatuṣpathē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — in prison or on a journey, in battle, in the danger of enemies — in water, on land, in the sky, in vehicles, at crossroads — (9)

contentBlocks.original:
गजसिंह महाव्याघ्र चोर भीषण कानने ।
ये स्मरन्ति हनूमन्तं तेषां नास्ति विपत् क्वचित् ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gajasiṃha mahāvyāghra chōra bhīṣaṇa kānanē ।
yē smaranti hanūmantaṃ tēṣāṃ nāsti vipat kvachit ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: — wherever those who remember Hanumān amid terrible forests of elephants, lions, great tigers and fearsome thieves — for them calamity exists nowhere. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्ववानरमुख्यानां प्राणभूतात्मने नमः ।
शरण्याय वरेण्याय वायुपुत्राय ते नमः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvavānaramukhyānāṃ prāṇabhūtātmanē namaḥ ।
śaraṇyāya varēṇyāya vāyuputrāya tē namaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one who is the very life and soul of all the chief monkeys — to the refuge, to the most worthy — salutation to you, O son of Vāyu. (11)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रदोषे वा प्रभाते वा ये स्मरन्त्यञ्जनासुतम् ।
अर्थसिद्धिं जयं कीर्तिं प्राप्नुवन्ति न संशयः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pradōṣē vā prabhātē vā yē smarantyañjanāsutam ।
arthasiddhiṃ jayaṃ kīrtiṃ prāpnuvanti na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who remember the son of Añjanā at evening or at dawn — they attain the accomplishment of their purpose, victory, and renown — without doubt. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
जप्त्वा स्तोत्रमिदं मन्त्रं प्रतिवारं पठेन्नरः ।
राजस्थाने सभास्थाने प्राप्ते वादे लभेज्जयम् ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
japtvā stōtramidaṃ mantraṃ prativāraṃ paṭhēnnaraḥ ।
rājasthānē sabhāsthānē prāptē vādē labhējjayam ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The person who recites this stotram-mantra each time — in the royal court, in an assembly, when a debate arises — attains victory. (13)

contentBlocks.original:
विभीषणकृतं स्तोत्रं यः पठेत् प्रयतो नरः ।
सर्वापद्भ्यो विमुच्येत नाऽत्र कार्या विचारणा ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vibhīṣaṇakṛtaṃ stōtraṃ yaḥ paṭhēt prayatō naraḥ ।
sarvāpadbhyō vimuchyēta nā'tra kāryā vichāraṇā ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The devout person who recites this stotram composed by Vibhīṣaṇa is freed from all calamities — there is no need to deliberate otherwise. (14)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्त्रः ।
मर्कटेश महोत्साह सर्वशोकनिवारक ।
शत्रून् संहर मां रक्ष श्रियं दापय भो हरे ॥ 15
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mantraḥ ।
markaṭēśa mahōtsāha sarvaśōkanivāraka ।
śatrūn saṃhara māṃ rakṣa śriyaṃ dāpaya bhō harē ॥ 15
contentBlocks.translation: Mantra: O Lord of monkeys, O greatly enthusiastic one, O remover of all sorrow — slay my enemies, protect me, O Hari, and grant me prosperity. (15)
Thus ends the Sarvāpaduddhāraka Śrī Hanumat Stotram composed by Vibhīṣaṇa.
